data.narrative:
Skyroot Aerospace is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $95M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of five private peers at the same stage and sector (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Isar Aerospace), alongside five public Space & Aerospace benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods apply to this target. Stage-progression analysis, using the $95M raised as a proxy for post-Series B valuation and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $162M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $1.0B (Isar Aerospace) to $2.5B (Axiom Space). Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 53.2x) cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure in the target's available data.

The triangulated range across applicable methods is $137M to $1.7B, with a median of $831M. This wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression suggests a markedly lower valuation than peer comparables, likely because raised capital alone is an incomplete proxy for operational progress, customer traction, or path to revenue. Key uncertainties include Skyroot's relative maturity versus peers, launch cadence, customer contracts, and unit economics. A narrower range would require disclosure of revenue, bookings, or operational milestones.
